title: Transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ofkp","text":"This protocol is designed to transform plasmids from distribution kits of iGEM 2017, 2018, 2019 as well as those synthesized by Mission Biotech with yT\u0026A backbone into E.coli DH5-α competent cells. Chloramphenicol is used for selection in transformation when the plasmids are retrieved from iGEM distribution kits, while ampicillins is utilized when the plasmid are derived from Mission Biotech. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cv3k6","text":"The ratio between competent cells and plasmid is 10 : 1. We will apportion 20 μL of competent cells for this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cdfeb","text":"Make sure that all the steps should be operated in laminar flow (except heat shock).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Turn on the UV light of the laminar flow for 00:10:00 to 00:15:00 in order to sterilize it.
Add ice into the styrofoam box and sterilize it again.
Set the dry bath incubator at 42 °C
Apportion 20uL of E-coli DH5α into an eppendorf. From step 6 to 8, perform them on ice and in the laminar flow.
Add 2uL of target plasmid into the eppendorf and vortex
Heat shock the eppendorf for 1 min at 42°C in a dry bath incubator.
Add 100uL of antibiotic-free LB media into the eppendorf.
Incubate the eppendorf in 37 °C , shaking for 01:00:00
Spread the transformed bacteria on agar plate containing antibiotics.
Place the styrofoam into the laminar flow.
Incubate the agar plate for Overnight, (16-18 hours)
creator: iGEM-CSMU
